text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data. In supervised learning, the computer uses training data that includes both inputs and correct outputs. By studying this data, the computer builds a model, which is a mathematical representation of the patterns it finds. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For example, a model trained on pictures of cats and dogs can then predict whether a new picture shows a cat or a dog. The accuracy of these predictions tells us how well the model has learned. Improving accuracy often requires better training data or more advanced models.
